inputs.note: 45-year-old male presents with weakness and dizziness. History of CKD and hypertension. Currently on Lisinopril 10 mg and Spinal Lactone 25 mg daily. Lab show potassium of 6.5, creatine 2.1.

flags.explanation: Patient is prescribed Lisinopril (an ACE Inhibitor) while documented with Hyperkalemia (Potassium 6.5). ACE inhibitors can further increase potassium levels, posing a risk of arrhythmia.
